name: The Winemaker's House — Jablan Winery (Rvaši)
description: TRIP'S MOST SPECIAL STAY. A 300-year-old stone house that IS the heart of Jablan Winery — Montenegro's only Demeter-certified organic winery — on a hillside above Rvaši on the NW shore of Lake Skadar. Listing: 'Imagine waking up, opening the shutters of a 300-year-old stone house, and seeing vineyards and meadows in front of you. No noise.' Guided tasting of small-batch organic wine made meters away, farm-to-table lunch, free mountain bikes, vineyard + lake-valley views, deep quiet inside the national park yet ~30 min from Podgorica. Sleeps 4 (1 bedroom, 2 beds — verify the bed setup for 4 adults). ★4.86 from 219 reviews (a heavily-proven gem).
communityNotes: Description (verified from listing): 'The Winemaker's House · Jablan Winery · Lake Skadar... the heart of Jablan Winery, Montenegro's only Demeter-certified winery... grounded and vibrant.' Independent confirmation (montenegropulse / mnevillage / argophilia): family-run hillside winery above Rvaši, wake to birdsong and wild herbs, evening organic wine on a private terrace as the sun sets over the mountains; complimentary mountain bikes. ★4.86 from 219 reviews. CAPACITY: 1BR / 2 beds = 4 max; check whether the second 'bed' suits 2 adults.
cost: ~€96/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Above the Lake (Bobija, Skadar)
description: Charming wooden cottage perched ABOVE Lake Skadar at Bobija (Cetinje municipality), with stunning open lake views, a big terrace for sunsets, and kayaks for rent to explore the lake's hidden coves. Genuinely scenic and private — 'where nature and peace meet.' Sleeps 4 (2 bedrooms, 4 beds). ★4.99 from 110 reviews — one of the highest-rated lake stays on the whole loop.
communityNotes: Listing (verified): 'Welcome to our charming wooden cottage Above Lake Skadar, where nature and peace meet. Enjoy stunning lake views, fresh air, and beautiful sunsets... a spacious terrace for relaxing moments... We also offer kayaks for rent so you can explore the lake and its hidden beauty.' ★4.99 from 110 reviews, 2BR/4 beds — comfortably sleeps 4 adults.
cost: ~€116/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Wild Beauty House (Žabljak Crnojevića, boat-only)
description: Riverside house at Žabljak Crnojevića on Lake Skadar that can be reached ONLY by boat — total isolation and quiet, 200 m from the lake, with free kayaks and a local fresh-fish restaurant just across the river. A standout 'arrive by water' experience for the adventurous. Sleeps 4 but only 2 real beds (1 bedroom) — FLAG: verify the sleeping setup for 4 adults. ★4.99 from 83 reviews.
communityNotes: Listing (verified): 'The place is very quiet and calm, and it can be approached only by boat what makes it unique. For our guests, kayaks are free to use during their stay. Restaurant with local fresh fish is just on the other side of the river... located on the river side, just 200 meters from lake.' ★4.99/83. CAPACITY: sleeps 4 but 1BR / 2 beds — confirm bedding before booking for 4 adults.
cost: ~€116/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Cold River Treehouse with private sand beach (Buna/Bunica)
description: A treehouse on the bank of the crystal-clear river Bunica at 'Cold River' camp near Buna/Blagaj (10 min from Mostar Old Bridge). Private sand beach, kayak to a riverside BBQ grill or paddle to a magical spring, hammocks, breakfast delivered to your treehouse. Romantic, remote-feeling nature spot that doubles as a base for Mostar/Blagaj/Kravica. Sleeps 4 (1 bedroom, 4 beds, private bath+kitchen). ★4.92 from 106 reviews.
communityNotes: Listing (verified): 'Enjoy the lovely setting of this romantic nature spot nestled on the bank of tranquil river Bunica... Cold River camp consists of four Treehouses... You can rent a kayak and paddle to River Grill for delicious BBQ (breakfast can be delivered)... lay in a hammock on a sandy beach.' Guest review (via Booking/Airbnb): 'Super cosy and unique experience,' feels very remote even though it's close to lots to see and good restaurants. ★4.92/106, sleeps 4.
cost: ~€112/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Kostela Stone House (Poplat, Berkovići)
description: Modernly restored old stone house in a rural area near Stolac/Berkovići in the Herzegovina hinterland, set in a large plantation of aromatic (etheric) plants with a spacious patio and a beautifully landscaped garden. Quiet, characterful, and perfectly placed on the southern Herzegovina line (Mostar → Trebinje). Sleeps 5 (4 beds: two doubles + two sofa beds). ★4.94 from 157 reviews — a heavily-proven gem.
communityNotes: Listing (verified): 'Modernly restored old stone house, in a rural area, surrounded by a large plantation of etheric plants. A spacious patio and a beautifully decorated garden are ideal for a vacation... The bedroom has two creations (180x200 and 160x200) and two sofa beds (90x190).' ★4.94 from 157 reviews. Also appears in the user's own Airbnb wishlist (taste signal) — now confirmed available with a ~€119 June price. Sleeps 5.
cost: ~€119/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Village House (Čvaljina, Popovo polje)
description: Traditional rural household in the village of Čvaljina on the Popovo polje plain, in the Trebinje/Ravno hinterland — hosts Beba and Dragan, a terrace with valley views, and the Ćiro cycling trail at the door. Walking distance to the 13th-c. Zavala Monastery and Vjetrenica, BiH's most important cave; ~25 min to the Croatian coast and ~1 h to Dubrovnik. A real countryside stay that beats any city-center Trebinje flat. Sleeps 5 (2 bedrooms, 3 beds). ★5.0 from 46 reviews.
communityNotes: Listing (verified): 'This rural household is perfect for holiday vacation and casual break. In less than 2 miles is Zavala Monastery (13th c.) and the cave Vjetrenica, the largest and most important cave in BiH... You will be warmly welcomed by the hosts Beba and Dragan.' Terrace view + 'Ćiro' bike trail. ★5.0 from 46 reviews. CAPACITY: 2BR / 3 beds — sleeps 4-5 (3 beds; verify if all 4 want separate beds).
cost: ~€82/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Mountain Cabin Suvodo (Bjelasica, Biogradska Gora)
description: A two-bedroom mountain cabin in the heart of Biogradska Gora National Park on Bjelasica, with a terrace and a 'beautiful view on the most beautiful mountain in Montenegro.' Deep-green, flower-meadow isolation — 'where you listen only to the swarm of bees and chirping of birds.' A serene alternative/extension to the Durmitor block (note: it's NE of Mojkovac, off the direct loop — attach a detour cost). Sleeps 5 (3 beds, 1.5 baths). ★4.98 from 46 reviews.
communityNotes: Listing (verified): 'Two bedrooms, terrasse, place for hang out with a beautiful view on most beautiful mountain in Montenegro... located in heart of National park Biogradska gora... imagine yourself on place full of greenery, flowers... where you listen only swarm of bees and chirping of birds.' ★4.98/46, sleeps 5. LOCATION: Bjelasica side (Berane municipality) — a detour off the main Durmitor→coast line. | Off-route: Biogradska Gora (Bjelasica) is 50km NE of our route. Beautiful park but our Montenegro arc goes Žabljak→Ostrog→Skadar, not via Kolašin.
cost: ~€82/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Mountain Star House (Mojkovac, Sinjajevina/Bjelasica)
description: A mountain house set between the Sinjajevina and Bjelasica ranges (between Durmitor and Biogradska Gora NPs), overlooking Mojkovac and the Tara river. An off-the-grid escape with local-specialty food on offer and big-mountain views — a candidate for the Durmitor→south leg if routing via Mojkovac. Sleeps 5 (3 beds). ★5.0 from 10 reviews.
communityNotes: Listing (verified): 'Located between the mountains of Sinjajevina and Bjelasica, the national parks of Durmitor and Biogradska Gora overlooking the city of Mojkovac and the river Tara, staying with us will give you unforgettable moments. With accommodation you can also consume local specialties.' ★5.0/10, sleeps 5. Newer listing (fewer reviews) but strong. | Off-route: Mojkovac is on the Kolašin/Bijelo Polje road, 39km east of our route. We drive south from Žabljak, not east toward Serbia.
cost: ~€94/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Eternum Glamping (Ninkovići, Durmitor/Žabljak)
description: A 'luxury touch' glamping unit at Ninkovići just outside Žabljak, set in open Durmitor highland with sweeping mountain and nature views — for the Durmitor night when you want something more atmospheric than a town apartment. Sleeps 4 but only 2 beds (2 bedrooms) — FLAG: confirm the sleeping setup for 4 adults. ★5.0 from 17 reviews.
communityNotes: Listing (verified): 'Eternum Glamping is beautiful place for finding peace in heart of nature. If you want to relax and enjoy in awesome view on mountains and nature, here we are. Our objects are made with best standards and everything has luxury touch.' ★5.0/17. CAPACITY: listed for 4 but 2BR / 2 beds — verify bedding for 4 adults.
cost: ~€117/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Magical cottage in the mountains near the sea (Krivodol)
description: A rustic-yet-modern countryside cottage at Krivodol in the Biokovo/Imotski hinterland, ~15 km from the Adriatic — a serene 'forget your worries' retreat with cozy living spaces and an outdoor area, good as a Makarska-Riviera back-country night away from the coastal crowds. Sleeps 4 but only 2 beds (1 bedroom) — FLAG: verify bedding for 4 adults. ★4.94 from 17 reviews.
communityNotes: Listing (verified): 'Escape to your idyllic holiday home nestled in the tranquil countryside, just 15 kilometers from the Adriatic coast. This rustic yet modern retreat offers a seamless blend of charm and comfort... unwind in the inviting bedrooms.' ★4.94/17. CAPACITY: listed for 4 but 1BR / 2 beds — confirm sleeping for 4 adults. Runner-up next door: 'paradise in forest close to the sea' (id 1054929404657769777, ★5.0/12, ~€117, also 2 beds).
cost: ~€112/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Villa Mare Jabuka (Žrnovo, Korčula)
description: A newly renovated village house in green, picturesque Žrnovo just above Korčula town (3.5 km from the centre, a few miles from the sea) — quiet, characterful, with lots of thoughtful detail and 3 private bathrooms. A relaxed island-village base for the Pelješac/Korčula leg, away from the harbour bustle. Sleeps 6 (2 bedrooms). ★4.93 from 28 reviews.
communityNotes: Listing (verified): 'Beautiful village house, placed on quiet and green area. 3.5 km from town center. House is newly renovated, with the taste and many details making this property comfortable and cozy... Žrnovo is picturesque village, just a few miles from the sea.' ★4.93/28, 2BR / 3 baths, sleeps 6 — comfortable for 4 adults.
cost: ~€111/night for 4 (June sample; dates are route estimates, not final)

name: Mlinica — stone cottage with jacuzzi (Brijesta, Pelješac)
description: A beautiful stone house in a completely quiet spot at Brijesta on the Pelješac peninsula, overlooking the whole bay, with a covered terrace, jacuzzi, garden and BBQ. A romantic, isolated Pelješac stay (oyster/wine country, near the Mali Ston bay). Sleeps 5 (2 bedrooms, 3 beds, 1.5 baths). ★4.91 from 65 reviews. NOTE: ~€155/night — above the ~€130 target but genuinely special if the group wants to splurge a Pelješac night.
communityNotes: Listing (verified): 'beautiful stone house with jacuzzi... located in a completely quiet and relaxing place, overlooking the entire bay. Two bedrooms, a living room, a kitchen, a bathroom, and a covered terrace with a jacuzzi and garden furniture... In the courtyard there is a garden and a barbecue.' ★4.91/65, sleeps 5. PRICE: ~€155/night for 4 (over the €130 budget — a stretch pick).
cost: ~€155/night for 4 (June sample; OVER ~€130 budget; dates are route estimates)

name: Grandpa Mate House (Dragove, Dugi Otok)
description: A character island house at the edge of Dragove on Dugi Otok — built in 1930 by 'grandpa Mate' on his return from New York and lovingly kept original with modern retro comfort, in harmony with its surroundings and near Sakarun/Veli Žal beaches. The standout Zadar-zone stay IF the trip takes the Dugi Otok ferry (Day 1 island option). Sleeps 5 (2 beds in the parse; verify). ★4.92 from 25 reviews. NOTE: ~€170/night — above budget and ferry-dependent (off the mainland route).
communityNotes: Listing (verified): 'Back in 1930, after leaving Dragove to work in New York, grandpa Mate built a beautiful house... Almost 90 years later we're following the very same path... retaining the originality of the house and combining it with modern retro comfort. Grandpa Mate House is based on Dugi Otok at the edge of the village Dragove.' Near Karuba/Veli Žal/Sakarun beaches. ★4.92/25. PRICE ~€170/night (over budget); needs the Dugi Otok ferry.
cost: ~€170/night for 4 (June sample; OVER budget + ferry-dependent; dates are route estimates)
